2604.03070.title: Credential Leakage in LLM Agent Skills: A Large-Scale Empirical Study
2604.03070.paper_summary: Third-party skills extend LLM agents with powerful capabilities but often handle sensitive credentials in privileged environments, making leakage risks poorly understood. We present the first large-scale empirical study of this problem, analyzing 17,022 skills (sampled from 170,226 on SkillsMP) using static analysis, sandbox testing, and manual inspection. We identify 520 vulnerable skills with 1,708 issues and derive a taxonomy of 10 leakage patterns (4 accidental and 6 adversarial). We find that (1) leakage is fundamentally cross-modal: 76.3% require joint analysis of code and natural language, while 3.1% arise purely from prompt injection; (2) debug logging is the primary vector, with print and console.log causing 73.5% of leaks due to stdout exposure to LLMs; and (3) leaked credentials are both exploitable (89.6% without privileges) and persistent, as forks retain secrets even after upstream fixes. After disclosure, all malicious skills were removed and 91.6% of hardcoded credentials were fixed. We release our dataset, taxonomy, and detection pipeline to support future research.
2604.03070.paper_summary_zh: 第三方技能擴展了 LLM 代理的強大能力，但在特權環境中經常處理敏感憑證，導致洩漏風險難以理解。<lb/>我們提出了這個問題的首個大規模實證研究，分析了 17,022 種技能（從 170,226 種 SkillsMP 中抽樣）使用靜態分析、沙盒測試和手動檢查。<lb/>我們識別出 520 種脆弱技能，存在 1,708 個問題，並推導出 10 種洩漏模式的分類法（4 種意外和 6 種對抗性）。<lb/>我們發現 (1) 洩漏本質上是跨模態的：76.3% 需要對代碼和自然語言進行聯合分析，而 3.1% 僅由提示注入引起；<lb/>(2) 調試日誌是主要的洩漏途徑，print 和 console.log 造成 73.5% 的洩漏，因為 stdout 暴露給 LLM；<lb/>(3) 洩漏的憑證既可被利用（89.6% 無需特權）又具持久性，因為分支即使在上游修復後仍保留秘密。<lb/>在披露後，所有惡意技能均被移除，91.6% 的硬編碼憑證已被修復。<lb/>我們釋放了我們的數據集、分類法和檢測管道，以支持未來的研究。
2604.03070.author: Zhihao Chen et.al.
2604.03070.authors: Zhihao Chen, Ying Zhang, Yi Liu, Gelei Deng, Yuekang Li, Yanjun Zhang, Jianting Ning, Leo Yu Zhang, Lei Ma, Zhiqiang Li
